q: Scientific research and the development of new tools and techniques have undoubtedly improved the ___ of our lives.
Biology helps us to understand the living world and the ways its many species (including humans) function, ___, and interact.
Fields such as genetics and evolution give insight into the ___ and can help shape the future.
The food that we consume is the results of ___.
Agricultural crops are broadly grouped into foods, fibers, fuels, and ___.
Myanmar's agricultural exports include ___, maize, blackgram, greengram, pigeonpea, chickpea, sesame, onion, tamarind, raw rubber, vegetables, and fruits.
Rice ($\textit{Oryza sativa}$) production results in rain fed lowland rice, deep-water rice, ___ and irrigated rice.
The traditional method for cultivating rice is ___ the fields while, or after, setting the young seedlings.
The zero-tillage technique is a new method of ___ cultivation.
Wheat ($\textit{Triticum aestivum}$) can be cultivated ___ a year.
Maize ($\textit{Zea mays}$) is a ___ plant that prefers warm humid weather.
Maize is a ___-day plant.
Among the ASEAN countries, Myanmar is the leading ___ producer.
Myanmar is the ___-largest producer of pulses after India and Canada.
Blackgram is scientifically known as $\textit{Vigna mungo}$ and greengram as ___.
Horticulture is defined as the applied science of cultivating and growing plants used for both consumption and ___ purposes.
The word “horticulture” is derived from the Latin words ___ and ‘cultura’.
In Latin, 'hortus' means ___.
Horticulture focuses on the use of ___ with a wide variety of mixed crops.
___ is the vegetable growing, dealing with the culture of non-woody (herbaceous) plants for foods.
___ or Fruticulture is the production of fruits and nuts.
Viticulture is the production of ___ (largely intended for winemaking).
___ is the growing and marketing of flowers and ornamental plants for floristry.
___ is the production and upkeep of turf, artificial and live, for use in recreation.
___ is the cultivation and care of individual trees, shrubs, vines, and other perennial woody plants.
Landscape Horticulture is the selection, production and care of plants used in ___.
___ is the management of harvested horticultural crops to determine the best storage and transportation conditions.
___ is a method of growing plants without soil.
Hydroponic growing allows for faster growth and higher yields than traditional ___.
In hydroponics, the roots may be physically supported by an ___ such as perlite, gravel, or other substrates.
Root exudates in hydroponics can affect ___.
___ serves as a model organism in plant science and genetics.
Aquaculture, also known as ___, is the farming of aquatic organisms.
Fish farming or ___ involves commercial breeding of fish, usually for food.
Mariculture or ___ is a specialized branch of aquaculture involving the cultivation of marine organisms.
Cultivation of marine organisms in fish farms built on littoral waters is called ___.
Oyster farming is an aquaculture practice in which oysters are bred mainly for their ___, shells and inner organ tissue.
___ is the farming of algae species including microalgae and macroalgae.
___ is a system of producing fish in combination with other agricultural or livestock farming operations.
The term “Livestock” refers to any ___, land-living animal that is raised to provide resources.
Livestock can be used as a source of milk, other dairy products, such as ___, cheese, butter.
Sheep and goats produce wool and ___.
Manure can be used as a ___ for fires.
Animals such as horses, donkey, and ___ can be used for mechanical energy.
The grazing of livestock is sometimes used as a way to control ___.
Livestock contribute to the conservation of ___ and genetic resources for food and agriculture.
All forms of living matter required food as the source of ___-sustaining energy.
Plant only is able to manufacture food through the process of ___.
Carbohydrates and fats are classified as heat or ___ producing food.
Body-building food is primarily ___.
The bacterium ___ helps in the formation of curd from milk.
Yeast is one of the simplest ___, used in the process of fermentation.
Microorganisms present in the soil act as a ___ agent, which assist in the production of compost.
The formulation of drugs for fighting disease-causing microorganisms is made possible because of the study of the ___ of those microorganisms.
___ is a name derived from Greek words, which mean drug and knowledge.
About 80% of world population is still using ___ product for their primary healthcare needs.
Salicylic acid was originally obtained from the bark of ___ tree.
The bark of cinchona tree contains ___ a drug used to treat malaria.
Digitalis is obtained from the leaves of ___ plant.
___, $\textit{Catharanthus}$ sp. is the source of two drugs effective against Hodgkin disease and some leukemia.
Morphine and codeine are extracted from ___, $\textit{Papaver somniferum}$.
The healing of human minor illness by using therapies based on medicines obtained from animals is known as ___.
Leeches, $\textit{Hirudo medicinalis}$, secrete ___ which helps to keep the blood flowing.
A molecule called ___ from the viper venom stops Angiotensis-Converting Enzymes.
___ are insect larvae that feed on diseased and dying flesh.
Spider silk is referred to by many scientists as ___.
Humans make ___, a hormone that inhibits bone loss, in the thyroid gland.
A protein in the blood of horseshoe crabs called ___ (LAL) reacts to all kinds of microorganisms.
Structurally, plant fibres are ___ cells and are parts of the plant skeleton.
Cotton, $\textit{Gossypium}$ spp., is the industrial crop of Myanmar for raw materials supplied to ___ industries.
Jute, $\textit{Corchorus}$ spp., is a tropical plant that grows best in hot and ___ climate.
___ is an agro-based industry that involves rearing of silkworm moths.
The yarn obtained out of cocoons is called ___.
Changing into adult form from pupa inside the cocoon is called ___.
Silk is called the ___ and is known for its qualities like luxury, elegance, class and comfort.
Wool is mainly harvested by ___.
The quality of wool is determined by its fibre diameter, ___, yield, color, and staple strength.
___ is an important plant product which ranks next to food and fibres.
Wood intended for heavy construction is called ___.
Structural timbers are obtained mainly from the ___ and from the heart of the tree.
The scientific name for Teak is ___.
The scientific name for Pyinkado is ___.
Agriculture is defined to produce ___ which maintain life.
In livestock farming, animals provide resources like meat, milk, eggs, and ___.
Freshwater culture fish species include Silver carp, Common carp, Nile tilapia and ___.
In hydroponics, nutrients can come from many different sources, including ___.
The chief ingredient of aspirin is ___.
Medicines for heart disease are obtained from ___.
Periwinkle is the source of two drugs effective against ___ disease.
Leeches can eat ___ times its own body weight of blood.
Silk is a continuous protein filament from the ___.
The thick sweaters which cover you up in cold winters are made of ___ from sheep.
Horticulture is a branch of ___.
Domain Archaea include multiple ___.
Arboriculture is the cultivation and care of individual ___.
The silkworm moth is scientifically named ___.
Wood has high resistance to electrical and ___ stimuli.
Wool is chemically quite distinct from cotton and other ___ fibres.
The four stages in the life cycle of silkworm are egg, caterpillar, pupa, and ___.
Prokaryotic cells lack ___ organelles.
